summary: Plan is structurally sound and correctly identifies the three scaling levers. Current-state analysis is accurate. A few gaps need addressing before implementation.
strengths: Correctly identifies that canvas sizing is already full-screen — no layout bug to fix
Three-lever approach (pixel ratio, point count, shader complexity) directly maps to acceptance criteria
Shader octave reduction (3→1 sin() calls) is the highest-impact GPU savings with minimal visual degradation
Context loss handling (step 5) addresses a real mobile crash vector not currently handled
Risk assessment is honest — shader branching caveat is valid

required_changes.detail: Step 4 mentions passing config to createParticleField, but steps 2–3 only cover pointCloud.ts and ribbonField.ts. ParticleFieldConfig also needs noiseOctaves/enablePointerRepulsion/enableSlowModulation fields, and its shader material needs the corresponding uniforms. particleField uses CPU-based position updates but still runs through the same vertex shader uniform pipeline.
required_changes.fix: Add src/visual/systems/particleField.ts to steps 2–3 file lists and files_modified

required_changes.detail: The plan lists pointWarp.vert.glsl and ribbonWarp.vert.glsl but all three geometry systems share identical uniform schemas. Verify whether particleField uses one of these shaders or has its own. If it has its own, add it to step 2.
required_changes.fix: Check particleField's ShaderMaterial vertexShader source and add to step 2 if separate

required_changes.detail: The plan acknowledges 'No functional change needed — current code is correct' and proposes adding a comment. This is unnecessary busywork that contradicts CLAUDE.md guidance ('Don't add comments to code you didn't change').
required_changes.fix: Remove step 6 entirely. The full-screen guarantee is already verified by tests T-036-04 and T-036-05.

required_changes.detail: Test says 'Already covered by T-025-22 but re-verified with new config shape'. Re-verifying an existing test under a new ID adds confusion. The config shape change is handled in step 8 where T-025-22 is updated.
required_changes.fix: Drop T-036-08 from the new test file

suggestions.topic: Use compile-time shader variants instead of runtime branching
suggestions.detail: Instead of uniform-based if-guards (which GPUs may still evaluate both branches), consider using #define directives and recompiling the shader per tier. Three.js ShaderMaterial supports onBeforeCompile or string injection. This guarantees dead code elimination.
suggestions.rationale: The primary savings come from octave count reduction regardless of approach. This is an optimization of an optimization.

suggestions.topic: Add a point cloud integration test (T-036-06 equivalent for end-to-end)
suggestions.detail: Existing quality integration tests cover particleField and ribbonField but not pointCloud. Step 8 updates existing tests but doesn't add the missing pointCloud end-to-end test.
